pairs.question: Who was Donald Loren Gile and what positions did he play in Major League Baseball?
pairs.answer: Donald Loren Gile was a utility first baseman and catcher who played in MLB for parts of four seasons (1959–1962) with the Boston Red Sox. He batted and threw right-handed and stood at 6 feet 6 inches (1.98 m) tall, weighing 220 pounds (100 kg).

pairs.question: How did Donald Gile earn the nickname 'Bear'?
pairs.answer: Donald Gile earned the nickname 'Bear' due to his large stature, as he was listed at 6 feet 6 inches (1.98 m) and 220 pounds (100 kg) during his baseball career.

pairs.question: What were Donald Gile's career MLB batting statistics?
pairs.answer: Over parts of four MLB seasons, Donald Gile had a batting average of .150, with 18 hits (including 3 home runs and 9 RBI) in 120 at-bats across 58 games. He recorded 12 runs, 2 doubles, and 1 triple.

pairs.question: How many games did Donald Gile play at first base in the majors, and what was his fielding percentage?
pairs.answer: Donald Gile played 31 games at first base in the majors, committing 4 errors in 224 chances, resulting in a fielding percentage of .982.

pairs.question: What notable achievement did Donald Gile accomplish in his final MLB at-bat?
pairs.answer: In his final MLB at-bat on September 30, 1962, Donald Gile hit a bottom-of-the-ninth, two-run walk-off home run to give the Boston Red Sox a 3–1 victory over the Washington Senators. This was his second hit of the season after going hitless in 34 at-bats prior.

pairs.question: Did Donald Gile ever play in the College World Series? If so, when?
pairs.answer: Yes, Donald Gile competed in the 1954 College World Series while playing for the University of Arizona.

pairs.question: What did Donald Gile do after his 1962 MLB season?
pairs.answer: After his 1962 MLB season, Donald Gile played minor league baseball in 1963 before leaving the sport entirely.
